Today is February 22, 2026. An armed man was shot dead early this morning after entering the secure perimeter of President Trump's Mar-a-Lago residence. According to authorities, what specific items was the suspect carrying when he was intercepted?
(A) A shotgun and a fuel can
(B) A rifle and a backpack containing explosives
(C) A handgun and a canister of gasoline
(D) A shotgun and a hunting knife
(E) A semi-automatic weapon and a propane tank
explanation: The article states the suspect, Austin T Martin, was "carrying what appeared to be a shotgun and a fuel can" when he was observed by the north gate.
model_output:
I'll search for information about this incident at Mar-a-Lago today.Based on the search results, I can now provide a clear answer to this question.

According to multiple authoritative sources reporting on this incident from February 22, 2026, the Secret Service said the suspect was observed carrying "what appeared to be a shotgun and a fuel can." This is further confirmed by the Secret Service spokesman Anthony Guglielmi, who stated the man had a gas can and a shotgun, and by Palm Beach County Sheriff Ric Bradshaw who said at a news conference that the man appeared to be carrying a shotgun and a fuel can.

The incident occurred around 1:30 a.m. when 21-year-old Austin Tucker Martin entered the secure perimeter of Mar-a-Lago. He put down the gas can and raised the shotgun to a shooting position when confronted by authorities, at which point he was shot.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Washington Post (https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2026/02/22/mar-a-lago-intruder-shot/)
- OPB (https://www.opb.org/article/2026/02/22/armed-man-shot-and-killed-mar-a-lago/)

Today is February 22, 2026. The United States men's ice hockey team has just won Olympic gold for the first time in 46 years. How long into the overtime period did Jack Hughes score the winning goal against Canada?
(A) 46 seconds
(B) 101 seconds
(C) 80 seconds
(D) 121 seconds
(E) 6 minutes
explanation: The article explicitly mentions that "USA scored the winner 101 seconds into overtime."
model_output:
I'll search for information about this recent Olympic ice hockey game.Based on the search results, I can see that multiple sources report the timing of Jack Hughes' overtime goal. Let me analyze the information:

Jack Hughes scored 1:41 into overtime to give Team USA a 2-1 win against Canada in the gold medal game. This is confirmed by multiple sources including ESPN reporting Hughes scored 1:41 into overtime.

Converting 1 minute and 41 seconds to total seconds: 1:41 = 60 + 41 = 101 seconds.

The U.S. claimed its first gold medal in men's ice hockey exactly 46 years to the day after the "Miracle on Ice" victory, which aligns with the question's statement about winning gold for the first time in 46 years.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NHL.com (https://www.nhl.com/news/united-states-canada-2026-olympics-gold-medal-game-recap-february-22-2026)
- ESPN (https://www.espn.com/olympics/story/_/id/48005562/jack-hughes-connor-hellebuyck-lift-us-olympic-hockey-gold)
- NBC Olympics (https://www.nbcolympics.com/news/team-usa-defeats-canada-overtime-first-gold-mens-hockey-miracle-ice-olympics)

Today is February 22, 2026. Authorities in California have confirmed that all nine missing individuals from the Lake Tahoe avalanche have been found dead. According to Sheriff Shannan Moon, what was the composition of the group of victims?
(A) Five tourists, two local residents, and two professional skiers
(B) Six experienced backcountry skiers and three local volunteers
(C) Six friends (including a pair of sisters) and three mountain guides
(D) Seven members of a ski club and two park rangers
(E) Four family members and five professional guides
explanation: The article states the victims "included close friends, a pair of sisters and three guides."
model_output:
I'll search for information about this Lake Tahoe avalanche incident.Let me search for more specific information about whether the group included sisters.Based on my search results, I now have clear information about the composition of the group of victims in the Lake Tahoe avalanche.

According to multiple sources, the nine victims consisted of six close friends who were "passionate, skilled skiers" and three professional guides. More specifically, sisters Liz Clabaugh and Caroline Sekar were among the dead, making them part of the group of six friends.

The three professional guides were employees of Blackbird Mountain Guides: Michael Henry, 30, Andrew Alissandratos, 34, and Nicole Choo, 42.

Looking at the answer choices:
- (A) mentions tourists, local residents, and professional skiers - doesn't match
- (B) mentions experienced backcountry skiers and local volunteers - doesn't match
- (C) mentions six friends (including a pair of sisters) and three mountain guides - this matches perfectly
- (D) mentions ski club members and park rangers - doesn't match
- (E) mentions family members and professional guides - doesn't match

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NBC News (https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/lake-tahoe-avalanche-victim-names-rcna260068)

Today is February 22, 2026. NASA has announced that the Artemis II lunar mission will no longer launch on March 6. What specific technical issue was observed by engineers overnight on Friday that led to this delay?
(A) A hydrogen leak in the filters and seals of the main engine
(B) A software glitch in the navigation system during the "wet rehearsal"
(C) A structural crack in the propellant tank discovered during fueling
(D) An interruption in the flow of helium used for pressurization and cooling
(E) A failure in the communication link between the rocket and Kennedy Space Center
explanation: The article states that "engineers observed an interruption in the flow of helium required for launch operations."
model_output:
I'll search for information about the recent Artemis II delay announcement and the specific technical issue that caused it.Based on my search results, I can now provide a clear answer to this question.

The search results consistently indicate that NASA discovered a helium flow problem overnight Friday that led to the delay of the Artemis II mission from its planned March 6 launch date.

More specifically, technicians observed an "interrupted flow of helium" to the rocket system, and engineers had detected an interruption in the flow of helium in the upper stage of the Space Launch System (SLS) rocket. NASA explained that helium is used to "maintain the proper environmental conditions for the stage's engine and to pressurize liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen propellant tanks".

The issue was specifically related to an interruption in the flow of helium during the rocket's interim cryogenic propulsion stage, which is used for pressurization and cooling purposes.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NPR (https://www.npr.org/2026/02/21/nx-s1-5722339/nasa-artemis-ii-march-launch-delay)
- Scientific American (https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/nasa-reveals-new-problem-with-artemis-ii-rocket-further-delaying-launch/)
- Fox News (https://www.foxnews.com/us/nasa-likely-delay-artemis-ii-moon-mission-launch-again-helium-flow-problem)

Today is February 22, 2026. President Trump has announced an increase in his proposed global tariffs following a Supreme Court ruling. Under which specific law does the administration intend to impose the new 15% temporary tariff?
(A) The International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) of 1977
(B) Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962
(C) The North American Free Trade Agreement Implementation Act
(D) Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974
(E) Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act
explanation: The article mentions the new rate is a "temporary solution under Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act."
model_output:
I'll search for information about President Trump's recent tariff announcement following a Supreme Court ruling.Based on the search results, I can now provide a clear answer to this question.

The search results show that following the Supreme Court's ruling on Friday, February 21, 2026, which struck down President Trump's tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), the administration announced new tariffs under a different legal authority.

The president claimed authority under IEEPA to implement the new global tariff under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974. This is further confirmed by multiple sources: Trump is implementing the new so-called global tariff under Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act, which is different than the law under which the prior tariffs were ruled illegal.

The 1974 law allows the president to institute a "temporary import surcharge" of up to 15% if he finds there are "large and serious United States balance of-payments deficits" or to "prevent an imminent and significant depreciation of the [U.S.] dollar in foreign exchange markets."

Those tariffs, enacted under section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, are in effect for 150 days unless Congress approves its extension.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Yahoo Finance (https://finance.yahoo.com/news/live/tariff-ruling-live-coverage-trump-attacks-supreme-court-imposes-10-global-tariff-then-raises-it-to-15-184403258.html)
- NBC News (https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/white-house/trump-raises-global-tariffs-15-percent-trade-supreme-court-decision-rcna260049)
- MS Now (https://www.ms.now/news/trump-hikes-global-tariff-even-higher-to-15-after-supreme-court-ruling)

(A) 53%
(B) 51%
(C) 90%
(D) 45%
(E) 75%
explanation: The article states: "An analysis released by a left-wing think tank, the Center for Progressive Reform, said that the White House has 'already initiated or completed' 53% of the policies in the document."
model_output:
I'll search for information about the Center for Progressive Reform's analysis of Project 2025 implementation as of February 2026.Based on my search results, I can now provide a clear answer to this question.

According to the Center for Progressive Reform's analysis, the Trump administration has initiated or completed 53 percent of Project 2025's domestic administrative policy agenda in the 12 months following the president's inauguration. This represents their February 2026 update.

In all, 283 of the 532 recommended actions identified in the organizations' Project 2025 Tracker have been put into action. This is an increase from their previous October 2025 update, which had documented 47 percent completion.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

(C) Celia Cruz
(D) Ismael Miranda
(E) Bad Bunny
explanation: The article states: "In 1967, at 16, he recorded his first album El Malo with Héctor Lavoe."
model_output:
I need to search for information about Willie Colón's passing and his first album "El Malo" from 1967.Based on the search results, I can now answer the question about Willie Colón's first album "El Malo" from 1967.

The search results confirm that Willie Colón has indeed passed away at the age of 75, with his family announcing he passed away peacefully Saturday morning.

Regarding his first album "El Malo" from 1967, multiple sources consistently confirm that Willie Colón recorded his first album "El Malo" at the age of 16 in 1967 alongside Héctor Lavoe. Together, they formed one of the most important salsa duos on the Fania record label.

The answer is clearly Héctor Lavoe, who was Willie Colón's vocalist and collaborator on that groundbreaking debut album.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

model_output:

According to police reports, the perpetrator's mother, Jennifer Strang, and the perpetrator's 11-year-old half brother were among the eight victims killed in the attack. Both had been shot before the perpetrator attacked the school.

The other victims included a 39-year-old female educator, three 12-year-old female students, and two male students, ages 12 and 13 who were killed at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School.

(A) President Donald Trump
(B) Attorney General Erik Siebert
(C) White House aide Lindsey Halligan
(D) Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche
(E) FBI Director James Comey
explanation: The article states: "Hours later, Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche wrote on X: 'EDVA judges do not pick our US Attorney. POTUS does. James Hundley, you're fired!'"
model_output:
I'll search for information about James W. Hundley being fired as interim US Attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia.Based on the search results, I can clearly see that Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche announced the firing of James Hundley on social media shortly after federal judges appointed him as interim U.S. Attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia.

Multiple sources confirm that Blanche wrote on X: "Here we go again. EDVA judges do not pick our US Attorney. POTUS does. James Hundley, you're fired!"

Looking at the answer choices:
- (A) President Donald Trump - Not mentioned as announcing it
- (B) Attorney General Erik Siebert - Erik Siebert was actually the previous U.S. Attorney who resigned
- (C) White House aide Lindsey Halligan - Lindsey Halligan was the previous interim U.S. Attorney who was replaced
- (D) Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche - This is correct
- (E) FBI Director James Comey - James Comey was one of the people prosecuted by the office, not involved in this announcement

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. President Trump has indicated he is considering a limited military strike on Iran. According to his statement on Thursday, within what timeframe will the world find out if a deal is reached or if military action will be taken?
(A) Over the next 10 days
(B) Within the next 48 hours
(C) By the end of the month
(D) Within the next two weeks
(E) Over the next 150 days
explanation: The article states: "On Thursday, Trump said the world would find out 'over the next, probably, 10 days' whether a deal would be reached or the US would take military action."
model_output:
Based on the search results, I can now provide a clear answer to this question.

According to multiple sources, President Trump made a statement on Thursday (February 19-20, 2026) regarding the Iran situation. Trump said during the Gaza Board of Peace meeting in Washington on Thursday: "You are going to be finding out over the next probably 10 days." Several hours later Trump told reporters on Air Force One that "Ten, fifteen days, pretty much maximum."

This timeframe of 10-15 days is consistently reported across multiple news sources including CBS News, CNBC, Al Jazeera, and Axios. Trump said Thursday that he would make a decision in the next 10 to 15 days on whether to attack the Islamic Republic.

Looking at the answer choices:
- (A) Over the next 10 days - This is partially correct but doesn't capture the full range
- (B) Within the next 48 hours - This is incorrect
- (C) By the end of the month - This is not what Trump stated
- (D) Within the next two weeks - This corresponds to approximately 14 days, which falls within the 10-15 day range Trump specified
- (E) Over the next 150 days - This is incorrect

The most accurate answer is **(D) Within the next two weeks**, as two weeks (14 days) falls squarely within the 10-15 day timeframe that Trump specified.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CBS News (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-possible-timeline-iran-strikes/)
- CNBC (https://www.cnbc.com/2026/02/20/trump-says-hes-considering-limited-military-strike-against-iran.html)
- Al Jazeera (https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/19/trump-suggests-iran-has-10-days-to-reach-agreement-with-us)
- Axios (https://www.axios.com/2026/02/19/trump-iran-strike-10-day-deal)

Today is February 22, 2026. The British Chambers of Commerce (BCC) has expressed concern over the increase of US global tariffs to 15%. By how much does the BCC estimate this will increase the tariff costs on UK goods exported to the US?
(A) Between £1-2bn
(B) Between £2-3bn
(C) Exactly £5bn
(D) Approximately $130bn
(E) Between £10-15bn
explanation: The article states: "The BCC estimates that the higher 15% tariff rate will increase tariff costs on UK goods exported to the US by between £2-3bn ($2.7-4bn)."
model_output:

Today is February 22, 2026. The US Supreme Court has struck down President Trump's previous global tariffs. According to the ruling, which specific 1977 law did not delegate the sweeping powers Trump claimed for imposing those tariffs?
(A) The Trade Act of 1974
(B) The Trade Expansion Act of 1962
(C) The International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA)
(D) The National Security Tariff Act
(E) The Reciprocal Trade Agreement Act
explanation: The article states: "And nothing in the law that the president based his tariffs on, the International Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977, delegated such sweeping powers to Trump."
model_output:
I'll search for information about this recent Supreme Court ruling on Trump's tariffs.Based on the search results, I have found clear information about the Supreme Court ruling and the specific 1977 law in question.

The Supreme Court ruled on February 20, 2026 (two days ago from today's date of February 22, 2026) that Trump's aggressive approach to tariffs on products entering the United States from across the world was not permitted under a 1977 law called the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA).

The six justices found that the law known as the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, or IEEPA, does not give the president the power to impose tariffs. Enacted in 1977, IEEPA authorizes the president to "regulate … importation" to deal with "any unusual and extraordinary threat" to national security, foreign policy or the U.S. economy.

The ruling was 6-3, with Chief Justice John Roberts and Justices Sonia Sotomayor, Elena Kagan, Neil Gorsuch, Amy Coney Barrett and Ketanji Brown Jackson in the majority.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NBC News (https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/supreme-court/supreme-court-strikes-trumps-tariffs-major-blow-president-rcna244827)
- CBS News (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-tariffs-supreme-court-decision/)
- SCOTUSblog (https://www.scotusblog.com/2026/02/supreme-court-strikes-down-tariffs/)

model_output:
I'll search for information about a Supreme Court decision striking down reciprocal tariffs and the resulting tariff rates.Let me search for more specific information about the current average tariff rate after the Supreme Court decision.Based on my search results, I found multiple sources discussing the average tariff rate remaining after the Supreme Court struck down the reciprocal tariffs. Let me analyze the information:

According to the Yale Budget Lab article "State of U.S. Tariffs: February 20, 2026":
- Without IEEPA tariffs, consumers will face an overall average effective tariff rate of 9.1%, which remains the highest since 1946 excluding 2025

The Tax Policy Center also confirms:
- The average tariff rate, absent IEEPA tariffs, is now 9 percent

The Breitbart article also states:
- The ruling wiped out most of that overnight, dropping it to about 9.1 percent

Looking at the answer choices:
- (A) Approximately 15% - Too high
- (B) Exactly 11% - Too high
- (C) Around 2% - Too low
- (D) Roughly 10% - This is closest to the 9-9.1% range mentioned
- (E) Above 6% - While technically correct, this is less precise than option D

The most accurate answer based on the articles is **(D) Roughly 10%**, as the sources consistently report the average effective tariff rate at approximately 9-9.1%, which would be described as "roughly 10%."

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

The search results consistently confirm that the four astronauts selected for NASA's Artemis II mission are Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialist Christina Koch from NASA, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen from the Canadian Space Agency.

(A) Includes Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael Collins - these were Apollo 11 astronauts from 1969, not Artemis II crew members.

(B) Includes Jared Isaacman and Steve Witkoff - these are not Artemis II crew members.

(C) Includes Steve Witkoff - this person is not an Artemis II crew member.

(D) Lists Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen - this matches the confirmed crew.

(E) Does not include Reid Wiseman, who is the mission commander.

The search results consistently show that OpenAI banned Jesse Van Rootselaar's ChatGPT account in June 2025 after detecting concerning content related to violent activities. However, the company did not alert authorities at that time.

According to multiple sources, OpenAI said that the account's activity in June 2025 didn't meet the "higher threshold required" to refer it to law enforcement. The threshold, according to the company, is that the case involves an "imminent and credible risk" of serious physical harm.

More specifically, the company said in its Friday statement that it did not notify law enforcement last June because the company did not identify "credible or imminent planning." The company's policy requires that for posts to trigger a referral to law enforcement, they must indicate "an imminent and credible risk of serious physical harm to others," and Van Rootselaar's use of ChatGPT did not meet that standard in June 2025.

While about a dozen staffers debated whether to take action on Van Rootselaar's posts, and some urged leaders to alert authorities, company leaders ultimately decided not to make a law enforcement referral.

Today is February 22, 2026. The firing of James W. Hundley is linked to a conflict over the Trump administration's attempted prosecutions. Who was the former acting attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia who resigned after deciding not to bring charges against Letitia James?
(A) James W. Hundley
(B) Todd Blanche
(C) Lindsey Halligan
(D) Erik Siebert
(E) James Comey
explanation: The article states: "Hundley was picked to fill the seat vacated by Erik Siebert, who resigned in September under pressure from President Donald Trump... Siebert's office ultimately decided not to bring criminal charges against James..."
model_output:
I'll search for information about James W. Hundley's firing and its connection to Trump administration prosecutions, as well as the former acting attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia who resigned over Letitia James charges.Based on the search results, I can now answer this question clearly.

The search results show that:

1. James W. Hundley's firing is linked to the conflict over the Trump administration's attempted prosecutions of former FBI Director James Comey and New York Attorney General Letitia James.

2. Erik Siebert was the U.S. Attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia who stepped down after federal prosecutors in Virginia found insufficient evidence to bring charges against Letitia James.

3. Siebert had told senior Justice Department officials that his office found insufficient evidence to charge James and declined to seek an indictment on mortgage fraud charges.

4. Erik Siebert resigned amid pressure from the Trump administration to launch an investigation into and bring a criminal case against New York Attorney General Letitia James.

5. Siebert was appointed as acting attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia in January 2025.

<final_answer>
(D) Erik Siebert
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. Trade negotiations between the US and Canada were halted last October. What specific event triggered President Trump to stop the talks?
(A) Canada's refusal to sell US wine and spirits on their shelves.
(B) The implementation of the Online Streaming Act by the Canadian government.
(C) A disagreement over dairy import rules during the USMCA review.
(D) A social media post by Prime Minister Mark Carney from Quebec.
(E) An anti-tariffs advert sponsored by the province of Ontario that aired during the World Series.
explanation: The article states: "...talks were halted last October by Trump, who was upset over an anti-tariffs advert sponsored by Canada's province of Ontario that aired on US networks - including during the World Series."
model_output:
I'll search for information about trade negotiations between the US and Canada that were halted in October 2025.Let me search for more specific information about the World Series timing and the Ontario ad to confirm the details.Based on my search results, I now have clear information about what triggered President Trump to halt trade negotiations with Canada in October 2025.

The evidence shows that:

1. Trump terminated trade negotiations with Canada in response to a negative TV advertisement about tariffs rolled out earlier in October by the Canadian province of Ontario.

2. The ad, which used the words of former President Ronald Reagan to criticize U.S. tariffs, aired during a World Series game.

3. On October 23, Trump announced that all trade negotiations with Canada were canceled after the Ontario government published a public service announcement with audio clips of former US president Ronald Reagan stating that tariffs on foreign goods are poor policy in the long term.

Looking at the answer choices:
- (A) refers to wine and spirits - not mentioned
- (B) refers to the Online Streaming Act - not mentioned
- (C) refers to dairy import rules during USMCA review - not mentioned
- (D) refers to a social media post by PM Mark Carney from Quebec - not mentioned
- (E) refers to an anti-tariffs advert sponsored by the province of Ontario that aired during the World Series - this matches the evidence

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
